description: Vendored SGLang CuTe-DSL baseline (verbatim jit_kernel snapshot) behind a sys.modules alias + local entry ABI; two-process parity vs the real SGLang public ops: 39/39 unique captured signatures bitwise-identical.

evidence.correctness: parity PASS 39/39 bitwise (REMOTE_KDA_DIR/logs/parity_compare.log); harness baseline-mode 115 passed

notes: Baseline copy is the fallback target and the immutable benchmark reference.

description: Native CUDA port: one CTA per row, 256-bit AlignedVector loads/stores (16xbf16/thread, block=D/16), fp32 single-pass fused sum+sumsq statistics (one CTA reduction round, var=E[x2]-mean2 clamped, two-pass available via flag), operand classes scalar/row/per-token for scale/shift/gate, fp32 [D] weight/bias path, residual+gate fused single pass with res_out rounded to x dtype before norm (baseline numerics contract), fp32 epilogue y=norm*(1+scale)+shift, fail-closed stride-classified dispatcher with vendored-baseline fallback; built via snapshot load_jit (sm_100, -std=c++20 -O3 --expt-relaxed-constexpr, no fast-math); PDL off.

evidence.correctness: candidate-mode harness 117 passed (39 production sigs native, zero fallbacks across 9399 calls; regression grid native+fallback; negative probes)
evidence.benchmark_csv: benchmark.csv run_id=r0-v1: geomean over 39 unique signatures 1.7037x endtoend / 1.5985x device-events

notes: Surprises to profile: per-token fp32 scale/shift rows are device-parity-or-slower (helios 104.8->113.1us); huge bf16 rows reach ~4.9TB/s of ~8TB/s peak. Event-based device mode measures stream-span (includes issue starvation on tiny shapes) - NCU owns the true kernel-duration numbers.

description: v2 = Codex-audit fixes + NCU-round-1 specialization: (a) TWO_PASS_VARIANCE=True (contract-exact layer statistics; fused E[x2]-mean2 removed from shipped path); (b) dispatch guards hardened (is_cuda/same-device, numel>0, non-tensor scale/shift fall back); (c) per-combo vector width: fp32-operand combos use kVecBytes=16 (block=D/8) after NCU showed occ 41%/long_scoreboard 16.4 vs baseline occ 82% on token-fp32, bf16-only combos keep kVecBytes=32; (d) local kda_nss::* custom-op layer (shipping_entry_points) so A/B compares IDENTICAL host stacks; (e) contract-exact srnss fp32 reference (pre-norm cast) + adversarial/cpu/empty/None probes (121 tests).

evidence.benchmark_csv: run_id=r1-v2: geomean 39 unique sigs 1.3090x endtoend / 1.2879x device (SYMMETRIC custom-op layers both sides; v1's 1.70x included inadmissible host-layer asymmetry); token-fp32 buckets recovered to 0.98-1.05x; big bf16 rows 1.345x

notes: Two-pass costs ~7% on huge rows vs v1 single-pass; accepted for contract exactness.

description: Single-round Welford/Chan parallel-merge layer statistics (thread-local mean/M2 over registers + 3-float butterfly merge). Numerically robust and correct (121 tests passed), but SLOWER than two-pass: the merge's division inside the dependent shuffle chain plus 3-float payload outweighs the saved reduction round.
source_hash: (kept in-tree as dead branch under TWO_PASS_VARIANCE=True)

evidence.benchmark_csv: run_id=r2-v3: geomean 1.1615x endtoend / 1.1063x device; s176400 1007.9us vs two-pass 788.8us vs baseline 1061.4us

sources: Chan et al. parallel variance merge
notes: REJECTED on r2-v3 evidence; shipped config reverts to two-pass. A future equal-count specialization (division-free warp phase) was considered and deprioritized: expected recovery ~4-5% on huge rows only.

description: Shipped configuration: cand-0002 config (two-pass variance, per-combo vec width 32B bf16 / 16B fp32-operand combos, hardened guards, symmetric custom-op layer) with the rejected Welford path retained as a documented dead branch. Final source hash bound to run r3-final.

evidence.correctness: candidate-mode 121 passed (39 production sigs native, zero fallbacks; grid + negative probes)
evidence.benchmark_csv: run_id=r3-final: geomean 39 unique signatures 1.2987x endtoend / 1.2896x device; per-bucket table in docs/dispatch.md; roofline in docs/results.md

notes: Per-token fp32 buckets at DRAM operand-stream bound (parity, documented no-go); nss bf16 broadcast family mixed instruction/DRAM bound at ~57-60% peak with the packed-cvt lever recorded for future rounds.

description: Audit-driven re-validation + in-SGLang drop-in arbiter for cand-0004. (a) r3-final discarded: evidence audit caught GPU-0 contamination in its own idle columns; r4-final re-run on verified-idle GPU1 with all-GPU before/after snapshots -> geomean 1.3070x endtoend / 1.2905x device over 39 unique signatures (twin-shape consistency restored: s27030 1.489x vs s27085 1.481x). (b) candidate_src_hash now binds .cuh+wrapper.py+register.py jointly. (c) Final-config NCU on 3 buckets (profile/r4f-*): huge-bf16 mixed instruction/DRAM bound confirmed (sm_SOL 59.8>mem_SOL 41.9, barrier 3.01 = two-pass cost); token-fp32 vec16 fix verified at hardware level (regs 52->32, occ 41->79%, mem_SOL 71%); tiny s47 kernel 7.3us locked = host/launch floor proven. (d) In-SGLang drop-in (docs/sglang_jit_export.md): official upstream grid 147 passed on the patched checkout (registration untouched), symmetric same-op A/B 1.108-1.448x on six buckets, rms fallback bitwise.

evidence.correctness: in-SGLang official grid 147 passed; kernel-folder harness 121 passed

notes: Promotion evidence package complete: bound-or-no-go satisfied per bucket; shipping integration preserves the custom-op contract.
